item.title: Investors pile into cash on fears of Iran war fallout
item.description: Markets left with ‘few places to hide’ from disruption caused by conflict

item.title: Want to claim medical expenses on your taxes? Read this first
item.description: Experts say it requires strategy, from figuring out the most efficient way for households to put expenses through to determining what can be claimed province by province

item.description: UK, Netherlands and Finland join forces to reduce procurement costs through economies of scale

item.title: Consumers to bear the brunt as soaring fuel prices hit shipping industry, experts say
item.description: Container shipping rates have started to rise, increasing 12 per cent in the two weeks ended last Thursday

item.title: Warner Bros chief David Zaslav in line for $700mn payday
item.description: One of Hollywood’s highest earners stands to reap huge rewards from Paramount’s $111bn takeover of Warner Bros Discovery

item.description: ‘When our clients call us, it’s not just a transaction, it’s a relationship,’ says Mitri Dahdaly, president Cullitons Limousine Service

item.description: Experts say companies need to rethink how work gets measured and be on the lookout for burnout

item.description: Arsenio Dominguez does not believe military protection for ships is a sustainable solution

item.description: Energy minister says suspending ETS in response to oil price surge would be a ‘big error’

item.description: There is a case for bosses to accept that market forces, like bad weather, just have to be navigated as they arise

item.description: Lack of sufficient cover means some investors are walking away from deals

item.description: The country is a canary in the coal mine for dealing with the strains of a boom in energy demand

item.description: ‘Disconnection’ leaves 11mn without power in largest outage since US energy blockade began earlier this year

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sees $1-trillion of AI chip revenue opportunity through 2027
item.description: Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang said on Monday that the revenue opportunity for its artificial intelligence chips may reach at least $1-trillion through 2027, as the company outlined a strategy to compete more aggressively in the fast-growing market for running AI systems in real time.

item.description: Younger people hoping to upsize for a growing family face bigger challenge than last generation

item.description: Shortages of fuels and chemicals threaten industries from farming to pharmaceuticals

item.description: Karen Hogan gave solid marks to Crown corporation’s management practices, but says Via has failed to improve its on-time performance

item.description: Expecting a new agency with around 150 employees to assume the mandate is impractical and naive

item.description: When friendly countries are clamouring for energy, resource-rich Canada can offer nothing

item.description: Our economy remains highly fossil fuel-dependent partly because we are not electrifying in the desired way Martin Wolf

item.title: Housing affordability has been improving. It’s not enough and may not last much longer
item.description: The conflict in Iran and the prospect of rising interest rates could bring higher borrowing costs
